behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the vision model’s real classification judgment on explicit content. A stubbed classifier would hide misses/false negatives on actual explicit imagery, so Integration is too low. System was considered but rejected because the behavior text targets classification correctness ("explicit content is flagged"), not OS/network mechanics. Workflow was considered but rejected because this behavior is governed by a single model decision path, not multiple agentic decisions chained.
behaviors.test_description: Run real image submissions (including explicit-content fixtures) through the moderation service using the production vision model path, then assert flagged outputs are routed to hold-for-review and never published.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real text-model robustness to adversarial/obfuscated hate speech. Mocked outputs would always pass and cannot reveal model blind spots. System was considered but rejected because the core risk is model output quality (detecting obfuscation), not transport/OS behavior. Workflow was considered but rejected because the behavior centers on one model’s classification decision.
behaviors.test_description: Send a corpus of obfuscated hate-speech variants through the live text moderation model path and assert each is flagged and held for review.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: The failure appears at multi-step handoff: image decision + text decision + final publish/hold routing. With two model-involved stages sequenced, this matches a pipeline-level failure boundary. Agentic was considered but rejected because behavior text explicitly requires both checks and downstream publish outcome ("passes both content checks" + "published rather than held or silently dropped"), i.e., cross-stage orchestration, not a single model decision.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end submissions that include both image and text inputs through live moderation stages, verify both checks return pass, then assert the final state is published (not held, not dropped) with durable publish record.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end latency SLO involving real service execution, queueing, network hops, and model API round trips; in-process fakes can hide real timeout/latency behavior. Workflow was considered but rejected because the asserted correctness is timing ("completes within 5 seconds"), not quality of multiple model decisions. Agentic was considered but rejected because output content quality is not under test.
behaviors.test_description: Run timed end-to-end submissions against the deployed moderation stack under representative peak-hour load and measure submit-to-final-decision latency; fail if p95 (or specified metric) exceeds 5 seconds.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is module interaction: vision-client failure signal must propagate to retry-queue logic and must not fall through to auto-approve. This can be caught with in-process wiring (service + client adapter + queue/repo) using deterministic unavailable errors. Integration brake: (a) no real OS/network required if unavailability is injected at module boundary, (b) no dependence on model output quality, (c) no multi-agentic chain, (d) no LLM decision handoff correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Wire moderation service with a failing vision-client stub that returns unavailable/timeouts, use real queue/repository modules locally, submit image posts, and assert items are enqueued for retry with no publish/approve action.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: Consistency on borderline content is sensitive to real model variability/calibration; mocks would mask instability. The behavior also exercises short-window repeated real classifications where nondeterminism can surface. Workflow was considered but rejected because this is repeated evaluation of one model decision type, not a multi-stage agent pipeline with distinct model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image repeatedly within the target window through the live vision moderation path, then assert decision consistency rate meets policy threshold and routing outcome remains stable.
